steps.title: Fastest path: mark the angles first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all angles in any triangle is 180°. Given angle P = 104° and angle Q = 38°, we write: $$P + Q + R = 180^{\circ}$$ Substituting the known values: $$104^{\circ} + 38^{\circ} + R = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for angle R
steps.content: Subtract the known angles from both sides: $$R = 180^{\circ} - 104^{\circ} - 38^{\circ} = 38^{\circ}$$ Angles Q and R are both 38°, confirming the problem's condition that two angles are equal. That matches Choice A — 38°.
